2026-04-23T11:54:02.8461368Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T11:54:02.8461768Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T11:54:02.8462112Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T11:54:02.8462405Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

The README has one example for `ContainerGroup`, but `ContainerGroupProfile`, `ContainerGroupProfileRevision`, and `NGroup` — three of the four resource types — have no documentation or examples.

  "reference_model": "claude-sonnet-4.5",
  "token_class_weights": {
    "input": 1.0,
    "cached_input": 0.1,
    "output": 4.0,
    "reasoning": 4.0,
    "cache_write": 1.0
  },
  "multipliers": {
    "claude-haiku-4.5": 0.1,
    "claude-3-5-haiku": 0.1,

2026-04-23T11:59:11.5192040Z   
2026-04-23T11:59:11.5194323Z   Let me verify whether other provisioning libraries follow the same pattern of having just one primary example in their README or if they document all their resource types. Checking the READMEs for ContainerService, ServiceNetworking, Kubernetes, and PrivateDns, they each feature a single main example aligned with their primary use case, suggesting that covering individual examples for every resource type isn't the standard approach.
2026-04-23T11:59:11.5196884Z   
2026-04-23T11:59:11.5200229Z   That said, for a brand-new `1.0.0-beta.1` library with distinctive resource types like NGroup—which represents confidential containers and isn't immediately intuitive—having additional examples would genuinely help users understand how to work with these newer concepts, even if it deviates from the established pattern. I should check whether the test suite includes code snippets for these resource types before filing the issue.

Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"type":"object"},"name":"dispatch_triage"}]}}

2026-04-23T11:59:11.7130739Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T11:58:49.49032096Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/`\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57490) by @ArcturusZhang\n\n## What Changed\n\nPR #57490 added the initial `1.0.0-beta.1` release of the `Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance` library. It includes four generated provisioning resource types:\n- `ContainerGroup` — basic container group (has an example in README)\n- `ContainerGroupProfile` — reusable container group profile\n- `ContainerGroupProfileRevision` — versioned revision of a container group profile\n- `NGroup` — N-Group resource for confidential/scale container scenarios\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- No README example for `ContainerGroupProfile` — this resource type enables reusable container templates and is a first-class provisioning resource with no documentation\n- No README example for `ContainerGroupProfileRevision` — revision management of container group profiles is undocumented\n- No README example for `NGroup` — an entirely new resource concept (\"N-Group\") with no explanation or sample usage; the name alone offers no guidance to users\n- Only one test snippet exists (`Snippet:ContainerInstanceBasic`) covering just `ContainerGroup`; no test-backed samples are provided for the three remaining resource types\n- The \"Authenticate the Client\" section in README is empty (a heading with no content)\n- The \"Next steps\" section in README is empty\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Understand the existing structure\n\nThe test backing the current snippet is at:\n`sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/tests/BasicContainerInstanceTests.cs`\n\nIt uses the `Trycep` provisioning test helper with `#region Snippet:ContainerInstanceBasic` / `#endregion` to supply the snippet shown in the README. Follow the same pattern for the new snippets.\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\n#### 2a. Add a `ContainerGroupProfile` + `ContainerGroupProfileRevision` example\n\nAdd a new test method to `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/tests/BasicContainerInstanceTests.cs` (or a new `BasicContainerInstanceProfileTests.cs` in the same folder):\n\n```csharp\ninternal static Trycep CreateContainerGroupProfileTest()\n{\n    return new Trycep().Define(\n        ctx =\u003e\n        {\n            #region Snippet:ContainerInstanceProfile\n            Infrastructure infra = new();\n\n            ContainerGroupProfile profile =\n                new(nameof(profile), ContainerGroupProfile.ResourceVersions.V2025_09_01)\n                {\n                    ContainerGroupOSType = ContainerInstanceOperatingSystemType.Linux,\n                    RestartPolicy = ContainerGroupRestartPolicy.Always,\n                    Containers =\n                    {\n                        new ContainerInstanceContainer\n                        {\n                            Name = \"hello\",\n                            Image = \"mcr.microsoft.com/azuredocs/aci-helloworld\",\n                            Resources = new ContainerResourceRequirements\n                            {\n                                Requests = new ContainerResourceRequestsContent { Cpu = 1, MemoryInGB = 1.5 }\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                };\n            infra.Add(profile);\n            #endregion\n\n            return infra;\n        });\n}\n\n[Test]\npublic async Task CreateContainerGroupProfile()\n{\n    await using Trycep test = CreateContainerGroupProfileTest();\n    // add expected bicep comparison string here after verifying output\n    test.Compare(\"\"\"...\"\"\");\n}\n```\n\n\u003e **Note:** Inspect the generated `ContainerGroupProfile.cs` and `ContainerGroupProfileRevision.cs` under `src/Generated/` to determine the correct properties and `ResourceVersions` constants to use, and fill in the expected Bicep output in `test.Compare(...)`.\n\n#### 2b. Add an `NGroup` example\n\nAdd a method showing how to create an `NGroup` resource. Inspect `src/Generated/NGroup.cs` for required properties. Wrap the infrastructure-definition code with:\n\n```\n#region Snippet:ContainerInstanceNGroup\n...\n#endregion\n```\n\n#### 2c. Add snippet placeholders in the README\n\nIn `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/README.md`, under the `## Examples` section, add two new subsections **after** the existing \"Create a Linux Container Group with a Public IP\" subsection:\n\n```markdown\n### Create a Container Group Profile\n\nTODO: brief description\n\n```C# Snippet:ContainerInstanceProfile\n```\n\n### Create an NGroup\n\nTODO: brief description\n\n```C# Snippet:ContainerInstanceNGroup\n```\n```\n\n#### 2d. Fill the empty \"Authenticate the Client\" section\n\nFor provisioning libraries, authentication is handled by `azd` at deploy time, not by the library at runtime. Add the following content under the `### Authenticate the Client` heading:\n\n```markdown\nThis library generates Bicep infrastructure definitions and does not connect to Azure at authoring time. Authentication is handled by the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/overview) when you run `azd up` or `azd provision` to deploy the generated infrastructure to Azure.\n```\n\n#### 2e. Fill the \"Next steps\" section\n\n```markdown\n- Explore the [Azure Container Instances documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/container-instances/)\n- Learn about [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/overview) for deploying provisioning infrastructure\n- Browse the [Azure.Provisioning](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning) core library for shared infrastructure primitives\n```\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/README.md` currently contains all required sections, but the following are empty or incomplete:\n\n| Section | Status | Action |\n|---|---|---|\n| Getting started \u003e Install the package | ✅ | None |\n| Getting started \u003e Prerequisites | ✅ | None |\n| Getting started \u003e Authenticate the Client | ❌ Empty | Add content per Step 2d |\n| Key concepts | ✅ Minimal (consistent with other provisioning libs) | None |\n| Examples | ⚠️ Only ContainerGroup | Add snippets per Step 2a–2c |\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ | None |\n| Next steps | ❌ Empty | Add content per Step 2e |\n| Contributing | ✅ | None |\n\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next.\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 containerinstance` — injects `#region Snippet:` code into README placeholder blocks; verify the README diff looks correct\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 containerinstance` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm\n5. `dotnet format sdk/containerinstance/Azure.Provisioning.ContainerInstance/`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","title":"[ContainerInstance] Docs: Add examples for ContainerGroupProfile, ContainerGroupProfileRevision, and NGroup resource types"},"name":"create_issue"}}}
